data.name: checkBoxDrawGuideCircles.ToolTip
data.value: Draw circles showing the boundary of each diffraction disk

data.name: comboBoxSolver.ToolTip
data.value: Choose how the Bloch-wave equations are solved (eigenproblem or matrix exponential; Eigen or MKL library). Results should match, but speed differs; use Auto unless benchmarking.

data.name: numericBoxThread.ToolTip
data.value: Number of CPU threads used for the CBED (Convergent-Beam Electron Diffraction) Bloch-wave calculation, 1-128. Disabled when Solver is Auto.

data.name: numericBoxThread.ToolTip1
data.value: Number of CPU threads used for the CBED (Convergent-Beam Electron Diffraction) Bloch-wave calculation, 1-128. Disabled when Solver is Auto.

data.name: radioButtonCBED.ToolTip
data.value: CBED (Convergent-Beam Electron Diffraction) mode: each Bragg reflection becomes a disk whose internal intensity changes with incident-beam tilt.

data.name: radioButtonLACBED.ToolTip
data.value: LACBED (Large-Angle Convergent-Beam Electron Diffraction) mode: uses a wider convergence angle so a reflection is recorded over a larger angular range.

data.name: numericBoxMaxNumOfG.ToolTip
data.value: Number of diffracted beams (Bloch waves) included in the dynamical, multiple-scattering calculation, 1-2048. More waves improve accuracy but slow the computation.

data.name: numericBoxDiskResolution.ToolTip
data.value: Angular sampling step per pixel across each disk (mrad/pixel). Smaller values give more diameter divisions, shown at right, for finer disks but longer calculation.

data.name: numericBoxWholeThicknessStart.ToolTip
data.value: Set a range and step of the sample thicknesses

data.name: numericBoxWholeThicknessStart.ToolTip1
data.value: Set a range and step of the sample thicknesses

data.name: numericBoxThicknessEnd.ToolTip
data.value: Set a range and step of the sample thicknesses

data.name: numericBoxThicknessEnd.ToolTip1
data.value: Set a range and step of the sample thicknesses

data.name: numericBoxThicknessStep.ToolTip
data.value: Set a range and step of the sample thicknesses

data.name: numericBoxThicknessStep.ToolTip1
data.value: Set a range and step of the sample thicknesses

data.name: comboBoxGradient.ToolTip
data.value: Select the intensity gradient direction: Positive means bright is strong; Negative inverts the scale so dark is strong.

data.name: comboBoxScale.ToolTip
data.value: Select the color scale used to map disk intensity to display color (Gray, Cold-Warm, Spectrum, or Fire).

data.name: trackBarGamma.ToolTip
data.value: Set a gamma value. The larger this value is, the brighter the outer disk appears to be.

data.name: trackBarIntensityBrightnessMin.ToolTip
data.value: Set an intensity at which the brightness is displayed as zero.

data.name: trackBarIntensityBrightnessMax.ToolTip
data.value: Set an intensity at which the brightness is displayed as the maximum value.

data.name: radioButtonAllDisks.ToolTip
data.value: The same brightness range (Min/Max) is applied to all disks.

data.name: radioButtonIndividualDisk.ToolTip
data.value: Separate brightness ranges are applied to each individual disk.

data.name: trackBarOutputThickness.ToolTip
data.value: Select a sample thickness to be displayed.

data.name: textBoxThickness.ToolTip
data.value: Sample thickness (nm) of the disk pattern currently displayed.

data.name: label4.ToolTip
data.value: Sample thickness (nm) of the disk pattern currently displayed.

data.name: label6.ToolTip
data.value: Sample thickness (nm) of the disk pattern currently displayed.

data.name: label7.ToolTip
data.value: Set an intensity at which the brightness is displayed as the maximum value.

data.name: label8.ToolTip
data.value: Set an intensity at which the brightness is displayed as zero.

data.name: label1.ToolTip
data.value: The same brightness range (Min/Max) is applied to all disks.

data.name: label2.ToolTip
data.value: Angular sampling step per pixel across each disk (mrad/pixel). Smaller values give more diameter divisions, shown at right, for finer disks but longer calculation.

data.name: labelDivisionNumber.ToolTip
data.value: Angular sampling step per pixel across each disk (mrad/pixel). Smaller values give more diameter divisions, shown here, for finer disks but longer calculation.

data.name: labelGamma.ToolTip
data.value: Set a gamma value. The larger this value is, the brighter the outer disk appears to be.

data.name: label11.ToolTip
data.value: Select a color scale (Gray / Cold-Warm / Spectrum / Fire).

data.name: label3.ToolTip
data.value: Select the gradient direction (Positive or Negative / inverted).

data.name: label13.ToolTip
data.value: Choose how the Bloch-wave equations are solved (eigenproblem or matrix exponential; Eigen or MKL library). Results should match, but speed differs; use Auto unless benchmarking.

data.name: label5.ToolTip
data.value: Set an intensity at which the brightness is displayed as the maximum value.
